hard.next_step: June 25 2026: Rishi sent reply June 24:: Hello Varun, As we will want to remain as much as possible within the out of the box functionality of the Zycus solution, we want to model as much as possible our CLM processes to your default process. As already shared our legal processes require review. Hence already being able to use your default processes for Legal CLM will help with our modelling / review. June 4, 2026: Spoke to Danny - he think realistically August for the signature as the Head of Legal Nathan wants to have the project well prepared before - we have a scoping session 15/6 May 28, 2026: left message for Danny - will call again next week. May 6, 2026: Caroline called Danny who said Nathan has agreed and they have chosen Zycus. Waiting for instructions from SWIFT April 20, 2026: We have had our direct management review and will be reviewing the outcome of the POC with Nathan tomorrow. It will only be following this session that I will be able to come back to you for next steps according to the mandate we will receive.Hi Danny,I hope all is well. Just a quick check-in to see if any additional questions have come up or if any additional information is needed. We would be very happy to support in any way we can.
hard.ais_why:
• Product scope is iContract only ‚Äî a single-module CLM deal at Swift Legal with zero AI modules in scope (no Merlin, no Agentic AI, no ANA, no Merlin Intake). Merlin flag is false on this opportunity. Pure legal-tech contracting motion. The single-module no-AI scope is the strongest possible AI-Resistant signal in chunk 5.
• Vendor Selected stage since 23 Mar 2026 with active POC and contract finalisation through May 2026. Next_Step_History captures Legal & Compliance team feedback after the Jan 2026 demo, pilot phase Feb 2026, reference call requests for legal-contract Zycus customers (HOLT/CAT, GCash, BOC), and an ROI session focused on efficiency gains, audit trail, contract retrieval and template standardisation. Pure CLM operational benefits.
• Customer-stated benefits in Next_Step_History ('Phase 1 soft benefits, Phase 2 industry benefits', efficiency gains, centralization/standardization of templates) are operational legal-tech outcomes with no AI-led transformation narrative. X7_Details, Business_Objectives and Customer_Business_Problem are all blank and Description is null. AI Resistant rating confirmed at 3.

north_star_verdict.headline: Swift has selected Zycus for legal CLM (Nathan agreed 06 May) and is an EXPANSION under the already-established Swift MSA where Zycus is live in procurement, so exec access is inherited, not a gap. The deal is healthy and engaged: the 15 Jun migration scoping call sized ~7,000 contracts and Zycus committed to a phased proposal, and the buyer re-confirmed an out-of-the-box approach on 24 Jun. The single live issue is timing: the signature target slipped to August and the close date has been pushed five times (30 Apr to 15 Sep), and the migration pricing proposal that unlocks management sign-off is still owed by Zycus.
north_star_verdict.math: 71 days to close (15 Sep 2026, today 06 Jul). Vendor Selected since 23 Mar 2026 (~105 days in stage). Close date walked 30 Apr to 29 May to 30 Jun to 31 Aug to 15 Sep (last CloseDate move 09 Jun). Buyer named August signature (Next Step 04 Jun) then mid-July design start (15 Jun call, Hrishikesh: 'mid of July you can expect the design to start'). Path to close: Zycus issues migration + overall proposal (owed since 15 Jun) then a one-week on-site design workshop then amendment/order-form signature under the existing MSA then 3-week migration of ~7,000 contracts. Last two-way touch: 24 Jun buyer email on OOTB approach; last call 15 Jun scoping session.
evidence: Stage Vendor Selected, Forecast Commit, Amount $45k, Close 15 Sep (71 days). Buyer selected Zycus: Next Step 06 May 2026 'Nathan has agreed and they have chosen Zycus'.

Expansion under an existing Swift MSA: Hrishikesh (Zycus), 15 Jun migration call: 'this will also function under the master service agreement we have established with Swift' and 'we are already implementing solution in the swift procurement department' (procurement build is live), so executive/seat access is inherited.

Live engagement: 15 Jun scoping call (effort, ~7,000 contract count, migration approach, pricing inputs) plus 24 Jun buyer email confirming they want to model CLM processes to Zycus default (Next Step).

Timing is the sole real risk: close date pushed five times (Field History 30 Apr to 15 Sep) and signature slipped to August; the migration + overall pricing proposal Zycus owes since 15 Jun gates management sign-off.
No external CLM vendor in play; the only alternatives are the incumbent SharePoint repository (being displaced) and the buyer's own do-nothing/manual migration.

metrics.narrative: Swift's legal team holds roughly 7,000 legal contracts (Danny, 15 Jun: 'we only have a max of 7,000') spanning more than 20 years, stored in SharePoint with no CLM tool, tracked manually with no automated signature or renewal management. Danny surfaced a concrete data-quality metric: 'it's about half that we are able to identify to link' to sole tools, meaning ~3,000-4,000 contracts need intensive manual review. However no cost of manual effort, cycle-time, or CFO-validated savings figure has been articulated on any call or in Salesforce (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c all blank per prior read). Quantifying an efficiency/retrieval/audit value case is the key move to arm the management approval Danny must secure.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with Swift legal management: Danny said on 15 Jun the migration estimate was 'requested by management' and that the numbers are needed 'for our proposal to the management', and Next Step (04 Jun) names Head of Legal Nathan as the person who must have 'the project well prepared before' signing. Nathan is therefore the mandate/budget owner and signatory; Edmund (Swift, engaged on the 15 Jun commercial/NDA discussion) supports the commercial track. Because this is an expansion under the established Swift MSA where Zycus already sells into procurement, access to the economic buyer is inherited rather than missing, and Nathan not personally appearing on calls is normal for a mini order-form expansion, not an access gap. The move is to package the pricing/value case so Danny can convert the management review into a signed mandate.
sources: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Danny, Edmund)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's evaluation criteria are explicit. First, out-of-the-box fit: the 24 Jun email states 'we want to model as much as possible our CLM processes to your default process' to minimise customisation and speed modelling/review. Second, migration capability and accuracy: Danny probed extraction quality and Hrishikesh committed '90 to 95%' metadata accuracy with validation reports pre- and post-upload. Third, effort and pricing transparency: Danny and Edmund asked for the effort estimate, cost per thousand contracts, and overall implementation cost. Fourth, operational fit items surfaced on the call: clause-library extraction (with a caution to avoid 20-year-old outdated clauses), master-to-child contract linking, and DocuSign integration ('the purpose is that we implement Docusign Swiftwide').
sources: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Danny, Edmund, Hrishikesh)

decision_process.narrative: The process is a management-gated expansion, read directly from the call and Next Step sequence: the buyer down-selected and chose Zycus (06 May, 'Nathan has agreed and they have chosen Zycus'), then moved into migration scoping (15 Jun) to size effort and cost for a proposal to management. Danny frames the next gate clearly: 'the sooner we can go to our management to show that we will have an outcome' and the follow-up management question will be 'what will the overall implementation cost be'. Head of Legal Nathan wants the project 'well prepared' before signing, targeting August. Post-signature, Hrishikesh maps a one-week on-site design workshop, then UI build, then a ~3-week migration of ~7,000 contracts.
sources: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Danny, Hrishikesh)
SF Field History (StageName Shortlisted to Vendor Selected 23 Mar 2026)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting is lightweight because this rides an existing Swift MSA: Hrishikesh confirmed on 15 Jun 'this will also function under the master service agreement we have established with Swift' and that a 'smaller mini' agreement can be drafted, consistent with a single-module iContract expansion being an Order Form / SOW rather than a fresh MSA. Signature mechanics are moving toward DocuSign, which Swift is rolling out enterprise-wide (Danny: 'the purpose is that we implement Docusign Swiftwide'); today much is manually signed. The forcing function is the management sign-off plus Nathan's readiness bar rather than any legal redline war. What would create visibility: confirming whether the mini order form/amendment is drafted and whether any NDA/security step (Edmund flagged a 'similar process' to the procurement NDAs) applies before signature.
sources: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Hrishikesh, Edmund)
system prompt 2.9 (single-module = Order Form + SOW under MSA)

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is a large, unmanaged legal-contract estate: ~7,000 contracts across 20+ years sitting in SharePoint with no CLM, no automated signature/renewal tracking, and poor retrieval. Danny quantified the data problem on 15 Jun: only about half the contracts can be identified/linked to sole tools, so the rest 'would need intense manual review' and the worst case is 'we recruit a bunch of people to do it manually'. The pain owner is Danny and the legal team, with the migration/digitisation effort the buyer explicitly wants Zycus to de-risk via automated extraction and validation. Inferred (not yet stated) pain: audit/compliance exposure and countersignature tracking that a repository cannot provide.

champion.narrative: Danny is the developing champion: he owns the buyer-side migration scoping, relayed the vendor-selection decision (06 May), pulls Zycus toward the management approval ('the sooner we can go to our management to show we will have an outcome'), and coordinates the internal design prep. His advocacy is operational and access to management/Nathan is real, but there is limited evidence he is actively selling Zycus internally against inertia or pushing to accelerate the August signature; he is deliberately measured ('don't have Caroline immediately call me by when we can sign'). Edmund reinforces on the commercial side. To harden the champion, give Danny a crisp proposal plus a one-page value case he can carry into the management review.
sources: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Danny, Edmund)

competition.narrative: This is effectively a sole-source expansion: Swift already chose Zycus (06 May) and is standing legal CLM up under the same instance and MSA as its live procurement implementation, so there is no external CLM vendor in the fight. The only alternatives are the incumbent SharePoint repository being displaced and the buyer's own do-nothing/manual migration, which Danny himself framed as the worst case. The wedge is out-of-the-box CLM automation plus a fast, low-cost migration that piggybacks on the procurement build team while it is still on the instance (Varun, 15 Jun: doing legal in parallel avoids procurement rework). The real threat is inertia/timing, not a rival.
sources: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Danny, Varun)

deal_movement.summary: Vendor selected in March, buyer confirmed choosing Zycus in May, migration scoping held 15 Jun, and the buyer re-confirmed an out-of-the-box approach 24 Jun. Momentum is real but the close date has been walked out five times and signature slipped to August.

items.change: Stage moved Shortlisted to Vendor Selected and amount raised $29k to $45k as CLM scope firmed
Buyer confirmed selection: 'Nathan has agreed and they have chosen Zycus'
Close date pushed 31 Aug to 15 Sep (fifth slip since 30 Apr)
Migration scoping session held: ~7,000 contract count, extraction accuracy 90-95%, effort/pricing inputs gathered, Zycus to prepare proposal
Buyer emailed to confirm they want to model CLM processes to Zycus out-of-the-box default process

competitive_position.summary: No external CLM competitor is in play: Swift down-selected and chose Zycus (06 May 2026) and is deploying legal CLM under the same MSA/instance as its live procurement implementation. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the buyer's own do-nothing / manual-migration inertia, kept alive by scope caution and a pending management sign-off, not a rival. The incumbent SharePoint repository is being displaced. An unnamed 'final two' rival from the 2025 down-select has faded with no fresh evidence.

competitors.name: Do nothing / manual migration
competitors.quote: the worst case scenario is that we recruit a bunch of people to do it manually, but can we do it much more smarter
competitors.how_we_win: Land the phased proposal and automated extraction (90-95% accuracy, validation reports) fast so management sees a de-risked outcome versus manual review of ~3,500 unidentifiable contracts.

competitors.name: Incumbent SharePoint repository (no CLM)
competitors.quote: we want to model as much as possible our CLM processes to your default process
competitors.source: SF Next_Step__c 24 Jun 2026; prior canonical record
competitors.how_we_win: Out-of-the-box CLM workflow, clause library, master-to-child linking and DocuSign integration the SharePoint repository cannot provide.

competitors.name: Unnamed 'final two' rival (2025 down-select)
competitors.quote: down selected to the final two; Nathan has agreed and they have chosen Zycus
competitors.source: prior canonical record (living memory); SF Next_Step__c
competitors.how_we_win: Already eliminated at selection; hold by delivering the proposal and locking the signature.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the criteria that matter to Swift: out-of-the-box process modelling, migration accuracy, and a piggyback on the existing procurement instance/MSA. The one open expectation is a transparent effort/pricing proposal for management.

items.quote: we want to remain as much as possible within the out of the box functionality of the Zycus solution
accuracy is as close as 90 to 95%... post upload also there will be reports generated

items.criterion: Transparent migration effort estimate and cost per 1,000 contracts + overall implementation cost
items.quote: if you could kinda let us know those numbers, like effort and maybe the cost per thousand contracts... for our proposal to the management
items.source: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Edmund, Danny)

items.criterion: DocuSign / e-signature integration extended to legal
items.quote: the procurement project where we are already integrating Docusign would get extended to your contracts as well

items.requirement: Deliver migration effort estimate, cost per 1,000 contracts, and overall implementation cost for management
items.quote: if you could kinda let us know those numbers, like effort and maybe the cost per thousand contracts that would help us with our proposal to the management

items.requirement: Provide the migration work-breakdown / Gantt sequence and the design-workshop standard steps and stakeholder guide
items.quote: I'll give you the work breakdown structure for the entire... in a Gantt chart style weekly wise... what would be the standard steps to come to a workable outcome

items.requirement: Confirm legal CLM can run on Zycus out-of-the-box default processes with minimal customisation
items.quote: we want to model as much as possible our CLM processes to your default process

items.deliverable: Provide total contract count (master contracts plus number of annexes) needed to price the migration
items.grounding_quote: I'll go provide you later today with master contracts and the number of annexes we have there

items.area: Quantified business case / ROI for management sign-off
items.quote: for our proposal to the management
items.source: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026; SF Value_to_Customer__c blank

items.area: Amendment / mini order-form drafting status under the Swift MSA
items.quote: we can draft a smaller mini... so that we can establish it

best_practice_check.summary: A won-account expansion where the win is decided and timing is the battleground; the levers are speed of proposal, a light value case, and paper compression under the existing MSA.
flags: Issue the migration + overall proposal now while the procurement build team is still on the instance; Varun noted running legal in parallel avoids procurement rework and saves time.
Attach a one-page efficiency/retrieval/audit value case to the proposal; AIS is low and no ROI is documented, and management sign-off is the gating question.
Position legal CLM as a mini order form/amendment under the established Swift MSA plus the Swift-wide DocuSign rollout to compress paper toward the August signature.

items.name: Nathan
items.title: Head of Legal
items.risk: Holds signature authority and the mandate/budget decision (Next Step 04 Jun: wants the project 'well prepared before' signing; 06 May: agreed and chosen Zycus). Not personally on calls, but this is a mini order-form expansion under the existing Swift MSA so access is inherited, not a gap; the action is to arm Danny with proposal + value case for the management review.

items.name: Danny
items.title: Swift legal project lead (mandate/management liaison)
items.risk: Developing champion: owns migration scoping, relayed selection, drives toward management sign-off, but measured on urgency ('don't have Caroline immediately call me by when we can sign'). Give him a crisp proposal + value case to accelerate.
items.source: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026; SF Next_Step__c

items.name: Edmund
items.title: Swift (commercial / contracting)
items.risk: Engaged on commercials, NDA and design-phase planning on 15 Jun; supports the buying process. Keep looped on pricing and the mini order form under the MSA.

champion_strength.summary: Danny is a developing champion: operationally central (owns migration scoping, relayed the 06 May selection, coordinates internal design prep and the management approval path) with real access to Head of Legal Nathan and management. What has developed: he drives the buyer-side process and pulls Zycus for the proposal. What is missing: evidence he is actively selling Zycus internally against inertia or pushing to accelerate the August signature; his cadence is deliberately measured. Not at risk given the win is decided, but strengthen by handing him a proposal plus a one-page value case for management.
champion_strength.source: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Danny); SF Next_Step__c 06 May and 04 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: This is a deliberate non-AI, single-module iContract CLM expansion; the Merlin/agentic narrative is not applicable and should not be forced. Positioning strength is correctly low on AI and that is fine for this deal.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 3.0 / Negative (AI Resistant): single-module iContract, no AI modules in scope. The 15 Jun call confirms a pure CLM migration and workflow motion with no AI-led transformation ask; the field read matches the calls.

items.detail: Close date pushed five times (30 Apr to 15 Sep) and signature slipped to August; 71 days to close with the pricing proposal still owed.
items.source: SF Field History (CloseDate moves); SF Next_Step__c 04 Jun 2026

items.detail: Management/Head-of-Legal sign-off is pending and hinges on a migration effort estimate, cost per 1,000 contracts, and overall implementation cost Zycus has not yet issued.
items.source: Avoma migration scoping call 15 Jun 2026 (Edmund, Danny)

items.detail: Roughly half of ~7,000 contracts cannot be linked to sole tools and the buyer's process is not fully documented, so migration data quality and internal design prep could stretch the timeline.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is engaged and progressing (15 Jun scoping call, 24 Jun buyer email), win decided; the only cooling signal is repeated date slippage and a pending Zycus proposal.

items.signal: Buyer chose Zycus and is actively scoping migration and design prep
Signature target slipped to August; close date walked out five times

critical_signals.text: Swift has chosen Zycus for legal CLM; the only alternative left is their own do-nothing/manual migration, not another vendor, and it was confirmed on 6 May.
The 15 June migration scoping call landed well: Zycus agreed to size ~7,000 contracts and Danny asked for a work-breakdown and effort estimate to take to management.
Pricing is not yet issued and the management sign-off hinges on the migration effort estimate and cost-per-1,000-contracts Zycus still owes.
The buyer wants to run legal CLM on Zycus out-of-the-box default processes to minimise customisation, confirmed 24 June.
Nathan, Head of Legal, holds the signature and the mandate but has not joined a call; access is inherited from the live Swift procurement engagement and runs through Danny.

items.action: Send Danny the migration effort estimate, cost per 1,000 contracts, and overall implementation cost.
items.trigger: Hrishikesh committed to prepare the overall proposal once Swift gave the contract count; buyer needs numbers for the management proposal.
items.expected_effect: Unblocks the management sign-off gate and keeps the August signature target alive.

items.action: Attach a one-page efficiency and audit value case to the proposal for Danny's management review.
items.trigger: Management approval hinges on outcome; no quantified ROI documented and AIS is low.
items.expected_effect: Arms the champion to win a fast management decision and de-risk budget approval.

items.action: Confirm the mini order-form path under the Swift MSA and lock a one-week on-site legal design workshop date.
items.trigger: Hrishikesh: work under the established MSA and target mid-July design start after signing; buyer wants NDA/SCA/MOU flows pre-worked.
items.expected_effect: Compresses paper and sequences design so signature and migration land inside the 15 Sep window.

contributions.evidence: Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 58 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 51 vs stage-expected 60.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 51 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 50 is anchored on the stage win (58), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 51, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Swift SC — Vendor Selected · $45K · closes 15 Sep
cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No external CLM competitor is in play: Swift down-selected and chose Zycus (06 May 2026) and is deploying legal CLM under the same MSA/instance as its live procurement implementation.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Send Danny the migration effort estimate, cost per 1,000 contracts, and overall implementation cost. (by 13 Jul)
Attach a one-page efficiency and audit value case to the proposal for Danny's management review. (by 17 Jul)
Confirm the mini order-form path under the Swift MSA and lock a one-week on-site legal design workshop date. (by 1 Aug)

expansion_context.prior_opp: Swift procurement / S2P implementation (live under the established Swift MSA)
expansion_context.note: Zycus is already implementing the procurement solution at Swift under an established MSA; legal CLM is an expansion, so executive/seat access is inherited. Do not flag no-exec-access or an unmapped economic buyer as a risk.

scope_change.detail: Amount raised $29k to $45k on 23 Mar 2026 as the migration scope firmed; scope has not narrowed.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer selected Zycus and confirmed the choice ('Nathan has agreed and they have chosen Zycus', 06 May) and asked for Zycus-specific next steps (proposal, WBS, OOTB modelling).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain confirmed (~7,000 manually-tracked contracts, ~half unidentifiable) but no quantified ROI or savings articulated.

calls_omitted: Trustpair / vendor bank-account (09 Jun, 28 May) - parallel Swift procurement/S2P workstream, different opportunity
iSupplier / iSource / iRisk / iAnalyze IAD walkthroughs (May-Jun) - procurement S2P implementation, off-scope for legal CLM
S2P access/security/governance and PMO calls (May-Jun) - procurement project, off-scope
Untranscribed CLM calls (Sync 11 Jun, Legal team demo 06 Nov, CLM follow up 01 Dec, BAFO 26 Jan, Zycus Demo 14 Jan) - existence noted, no transcript
evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Field history to 23 Mar 2026; activities/tasks last 120d; Next_Step__c trail Apr-Jun 2026
gaps: Head of Legal Nathan not observed on any call (access inherited via the existing Swift engagement; not a coverage-lowering gap on an expansion).
Many account-discovered Avoma calls belong to the parallel Swift procurement/S2P and Trustpair workstreams, not this legal-CLM opportunity, and were treated as off-scope to avoid blending two deals.
No quantified ROI/savings model documented in calls or Salesforce value fields.
